title: Preview · /ask answer with visualizations
summary:
## How the observability pipeline works

This is the **/ask** look — a read-only answer. There's no action bar and no plan to approve; instead the answer is markdown plus two self-contained **visualizations** (below), with the chat open for follow-ups. In the hub this run carries an **`ask`** badge.

**Short answer:** Claude Code hooks append events to `telemetry.jsonl`; the companion **server** reads that file and serves the dashboard endpoints (`/state.json`, `/data`, `/trace/feed`, `/changes`); the **dashboard** polls them and renders the tabs; optionally the server forwards spans to **Langfuse**.

See the infographic for the moving parts and the diagram for the request flow.

demo.intro: Two visualizations the agent drew for this answer — an **infographic** of the moving parts and a **process diagram** of the data flow. (In a real `/ask` you could comment on either.)

variants.title: Infographic — the moving parts
variants.caption: Hooks → telemetry.jsonl → server → dashboard, with Langfuse as an optional sink.

variants.title: Process diagram — the data flow
variants.caption: How a tool call becomes a span on the Tracing tab.
